key_insight: A thermodynamically consistent ion-exchange membrane model is built by combining site-occupation mass action with mean-field electrostatics, and extended to multicomponent, concentrated electrolytes.
engineering_relevance: The model offers a more interpretable basis for designing and optimizing ion exchange membranes for desalination, fuel cells, and aqueous batteries.
method: Review prior membrane models to identify key physical contributions and parameter linkages.
Derive a coupled mass-action + electrostatic framework along the polymer backbone.
Explicitly include multionic electrolytes and elevated-concentration effects.
evidence_signal: Comparison with experimental data shows good agreement for both static and dynamic membrane properties.
watch_item: Because several parameters are linked or redundant, careful calibration is needed to avoid overfitting or non-unique parameter fits.
paper_title: Thermodynamically consistent modeling of ion exchange membranes in multi-ionic environments
